notes.total: 152 tickets is a healthy quarter scope with strong completion (98%), suggesting good planning and capacity alignment.
notes.completed: 149 of 152 tickets completed demonstrates excellent execution and predictable delivery.
notes.completion_rate: 98% completion rate is outstanding and reflects disciplined sprint management and minimal scope creep.
notes.releases_shipped: 23 releases across 6 sprints (median 2 per sprint) shows consistent delivery cadence, though the last release date of 2025-09-25 appears to be a data anomaly for a Q1 quarter.
notes.oos_total: Zero out-of-sprint tickets indicates excellent sprint discipline and planning hygiene.
notes.oos_open: No open out-of-sprint items — target achieved.
notes.type_split: 19% bugs (29 of 152) is a healthy ratio, with majority of work focused on stories (73) and tasks (50), indicating good product development focus.
notes.avg_releases: Median of 2 releases per sprint with an average of 3.8 shows consistent shipping cadence, though the mean suggests some sprints had larger batches.
notes.time_logged: Only 8 hours logged against 2,191 hours estimated indicates a critical time tracking gap — team is not recording actual time spent.
notes.estimate_accuracy: Cannot assess estimate accuracy due to missing time logs (only 8h logged vs 2,191h estimated), representing a major planning data gap.
notes.no_estimate: 24% of tickets (36 of 152) had no time estimate, indicating room for improvement in sprint planning discipline.
notes.rollover: 8 tickets (5%) rolled over from previous sprints is low and acceptable, showing good sprint predictability.
notes.cycle_time: Median cycle time of 11 days is reasonable, though the mean of 23.8 days suggests some tickets took significantly longer and may indicate blockers worth investigating.
notes.assignee_workload: Workload is reasonably distributed across the team, with Gianni Toniolo (38 tickets, 25%) and Riccardo Fachin (33 tickets, 22%) carrying the largest shares; 4 unassigned tickets with only 25% completion rate need attention.
